principle: Payloads expose capabilities and state. Themes may rearrange them but must preserve action ownership and data attributes.

siteTypeGuidance.principle: Supported screens are capabilities, not mandatory content. In combined website/page + AppShell handoffs, include only the screens that match the brief, Kiwe settings profile, and site business model.
notes: A non-commerce news/editorial website should not automatically show cart, checkout, orders, downloads, or addresses.
A theme may support optional screens, but the preview and settings notes must explain whether those screens are visible, hidden, or only relevant for WooCommerce/member sites.
Notifications and AI are valid DSA surfaces when they use existing notification/AI payloads and keep action execution core-owned.

profile.ownedBy: PhoneKey, WordPress user, WooCommerce account when available
notes: Orders, Downloads, Addresses, and Sign out may be rows, cards, pills, or stat blocks.
Do not clone logout/account actions without their data attributes.
The theme may show counts such as Orders, Saved, and Kiwe points when payloads expose them; missing counts must degrade to labels.

cart.ownedBy: WooCommerce cart and DSA cart reconciliation

actions.authority: WooCommerce checkout route or DSA checkout surface gate

notes: Do not create an independent remove button unless core exposes a data-dsa-cart-remove contract; current removal is quantity-to-zero through data-dsa-cart-quantity.

FBT is always a side-scrolling rail. Themes may restyle its cards but must preserve .dsa-cart-fbt[data-dsa-cart-fbt] and .dsa-cart-fbt__rail[data-dsa-cart-fbt-rail] as a horizontal overflow rail with snap behavior; do not reinterpret it as a stacked list or auto-fit grid.
Checkout presentation may move, but data-dsa-checkout-open must remain on the authoritative checkout CTA.

forbidden: Submitting orders outside WooCommerce checkout contract

search.ownedBy: DSA Search REST and optional Bricks Filter Search bridge
search.currentFactory: assets/js/search.js plus shell placeholder in assets/js/surface.js
search.runtimeAdapter: visual-profile shell registry in surface.js; behavior remains in search.js

actions.authority: DSA Search REST and Bricks bridge

notes: Themes may rearrange the shell slots but must keep data-dsa-search-form, data-dsa-search-input, data-dsa-search-filters, data-dsa-search-alphabet, and data-dsa-search-results.
Bricks bridge reconciliation stays in assets/js/search.js; themes must not call Bricks filter utilities directly.

menu.ownedBy: WordPress menus, DSA menu settings, page heading context engine

actions.authority: DSA Surface close and page scroll bridge

notes: Page table-of-contents buttons are behavior-owned by the Surface close and page-scroll bridge. Themes may style or reposition them, but must keep data-dsa-menu-anchor on the clickable control and must not replace scrolling with theme JS.

saved.ownedBy: DSA Saved Items service and Woo wishlist-like state

notes: Identity, logo, site score, and commerce actions must consume Geometry Engine width and token colors. Do not let a score badge, logo, social link, Shop action, or Cart action shrink text below readability or become white-on-light / dark-on-dark in either color mode.

notifications.ownedBy: DSA notification preference service, Push/PWA journey, PhoneKey identity when required

games.ownedBy: DSA Games engine, reward session service, and Surface game lifecycle

actions.authority: DSA game lifecycle and reward session gate

notes: Themes may style the game shell, HUD, choices, messages, and canvas frame.
Themes must not replace the canvas engine, score loop, reward verification, attempt timing, or coupon issuance authority.
Standalone previews may use a static canvas poster or simple mock canvas; that preview code is never importable theme code.

ai.ownedBy: DSA AI insight queue, notification history, cart/notification/action executors
notes: Themes may rearrange AI presentation but must not execute AI actions directly.
Popout rendering and swipe/dismiss ownership remain core-owned.
